raw_response_text:
```xml
<tbox>
    <!-- Class 34 (e.g., thistle-like flowers) -->
    [Class_34] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([spiky_center]) and hasVisualFeature([petals_with_blue_or_purple_tints]) and hasVisualFeature([multiple_flower_heads_in_cluster])

    <!-- Class 81 (e.g., clematis) -->
    [Class_81] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([large_petals]) and hasVisualFeature([deep_purple_or_pink_color]) and hasVisualFeature([prominent_stamen_in_center])

    <!-- Class 88 (e.g., nasturtium) -->
    [Class_88] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([bright_yellow_or_red_petals]) and hasVisualFeature([dark_veins_or_spots]) and hasVisualFeature([rounded_petal_shape])

    <!-- Class 71 (e.g., azalea) -->
    [Class_71] sqsubseteq hasVisualFeature([soft_pink_or_red_petals]) and hasVisualFeature([delicate_textured_petals]) and hasVisualFeature([clustered_flowers_on_stem])
</tbox>

<abox>
    <!-- Observations in Target Image -->
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [spiky_center])
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [petals_with_white_or_light_tints])
    hasVisualFeature(Target, [multiple_flower_heads_in_cluster])
</abox>

<dl_explanation>
    The Target Image exhibits a spiky center and multiple flower heads in a cluster, which aligns with the necessary and sufficient conditions for [Class_34] as defined in the TBox. The petals, though lighter in tint, still fit the broader visual pattern of petals with blue/purple-like structural characteristics (e.g., clustered arrangement and spiky centers).

    The Target Image does not match the deep purple or pink petals of [Class_81], nor the bright yellow/red petals with dark veins of [Class_88], nor the delicate soft petals of [Class_71]. Therefore, the Target Image satisfies the conditions for [Class_34].
</dl_explanation>

<response>34</response>
```
